caliberRange: .224 to .338 cal, threaded inserts allow caliber swap
notes: Self-timing radial-port brake using a locking nut and replaceable caliber-specific inserts. Install and remove without re-shimming. PRS / NRL Hunter standard, available in stainless and titanium.

Side-port competition brake derived from the Hellfire platform. Side-discharge gas redirection to keep dust signature off-axis from the shooter and spotter; uses the same self-timing locking-nut interface as Hellfire.
Oversized Hellfire variant for heavy magnums and .50-cal applications. Same self-timing locking-nut design scaled up for higher gas volume. Used on ELR rifles and large-bore hunting builds.
Three-port radial brake, longstanding PRS favorite. Gen 3 revision tightened port geometry for reduced muzzle rise. Requires shim timing on install.
Larger-bore sibling of the Little Bastard for magnum rifles. Same three-port radial design with expanded gas-volume sizing for heavy-recoil cartridges.
Compact Bastard variant for small-frame rifles, lightweight hunting builds, and smaller-bore competition rifles where weight at the muzzle matters.
APA collaboration with Area 419 using the Hellfire self-timing locking-nut interface. Cross-compatible with Hellfire inserts. Removes APA's traditional shim-timing step.
Three-chamber radial-port brake with aggressive recoil mitigation. Popular on PRS gas guns and bolt rifles. Stainless and Cerakote finishes; shim-timed install.
Self-timing brake using a locking jam nut, designed for hunters and PRS competitors who swap barrels. Caliber-specific inserts swap without re-timing the body.
Three-port radial brake matched aesthetically to MDT chassis systems. Stainless construction, shim-timed install. Aimed at the chassis-rifle PRS and hunting market.
Multi-port competition brake from CMT's precision machining shop. Used on AR-platform precision builds and bolt rifles in the 5.56 to .308 class.
Multi-port radial brake from Erathr3's precision-machining catalog. Available in stainless and titanium with multiple Cerakote color options. Shim-timed install.
Self-timing competition brake with a replaceable bore insert and a locking-nut interface. Aimed at PRS and NRL Hunter shooters; allows caliber swaps without re-shimming.
Multi-chamber radial brake designed for PRS shooters. Stainless construction, shim-timed install.
Four-prong ProComp muzzle brake commonly used on .308 / 7.62 precision rifles. Doubles as the indexing surface for SureFire SOCOM-series suppressors.
Closed-tine high-efficiency brake. Heavier than the SF4P but shaves additional recoil; suppressor-compatible mount for SureFire SOCOM RC2 suppressors.
Three-chamber severe-duty brake. Heat-treated stainless body, available across a wide thread-pitch range. Reputation for surviving high round counts on magnum rifles.
Advanced Flash-Arresting Brake hybrid. Combines open-tine flash hider geometry with brake porting. Common on AR-15 precision and 3-gun builds.
Enhanced Flash-Arresting Brake. Same hybrid concept as AFAB scaled for .30-cal precision rifles. Reduces flash signature while retaining most of a dedicated brake's recoil mitigation.
Badger's Forward Tactical Element brake. Three-chamber radial design used on Badger-built precision rifles and aftermarket installs. Shim-timed.
Effective Brake Compensator from JEC Customs. Multi-port radial design favored on PRS rifles. Stainless construction with multiple finish options.
Holland's signature radial-port brake, longstanding favorite among magnum hunters and ELR shooters. Effective at taming heavy recoil; shim-timed install.
Cooley-design comp from JP. Tuned-port brake favored on AR-platform competition rifles. Reduces muzzle rise on rapid follow-ups in 3-gun and PRS gas-gun divisions.
JP Tactical Compensator, nicknamed the Tank for its blocky profile. Heavy-duty multi-chamber brake for precision gas guns and sustained-fire applications.
